zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Hurst於1568年3月13日之前出生在英國貝德福爾亨洛福時，她的父親William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment38歲，母親Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer31歲。她於1593年6月18日與Thomas Rogers在英國貝德福爾亨洛福結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於從1621年1月到1621年3月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Hurst于1568年3月13日之前出生在英国貝德福爾亨洛福时，她的父亲William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment38岁，母亲Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer31岁。她于1593年6月18日与Thomas Rogers在英国貝德福爾亨洛福结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于从1621年1月到1621年3月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Hurst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived near a wood or wooded hill, from Middle English hirst(e), herst(e), hurst(e) (Old English hyrst) or a habitational name from any of the places so called, such as Hurst Green (in Mitton, Lancashire), Hirst (Northumberland), Hurst (Berkshire, Kent, Warwickshire), Hurstpierpoint (Sussex), or Hirst in Longwood (Yorkshire).
Irish: re-Anglicized form of de Horsaigh, the Gaelicized form of the English habitational name Horsey , established in Ireland since the 13th century.
German and Swiss German (also Hürst): topographic name from Middle High German hurst ‘woodland, thicket’; or a habitational name from a place so named in Westphalia.

variants.name: Herst
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name or nickname from Polish herszt ‘ringleader, chieftain’.

variants.name: Horsey
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from Horsey Pignes (Somerset), Horse Eye (Sussex), or Horsey (Norfolk), from Old English hors ‘horse’ + ēg ‘island, low-lying land’.

variants.name: Hearst
meanings: Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of some similar (like-sounding) original Ashkenazic surname such as   Hirsch  .

variants.name: Horst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Phares, Alois, Armin, Fritz, Hans, Inge, Manfred, Monika, Reinhold.

North German and Dutch: topographic name from Middle Low German hurst, Middle Dutch horst ‘undergrowth, brushwood, wild place’. More specifically, the term was also used to denote a crow's or similar large bird's nest, a raised area in surrounding marshland, or an area of uncleared woodland, all of which meanings could have contributed to the surname. This surname is also a habitational name from any of numerous places called with this word. Compare Hurst .

History: As a name of German origin (see 1 above) the surname Horst is established among the American Mennonites. Their progenitors were the brothers Joseph, Michael, and Peter Horst, who came to PA in 1731 together with their widowed mother. Among their descendants the altered form Hurst is also found.

variants.name: Hirst
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hurst  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

William of Orange (1533-1584), Prince of Orange (1538-1584), Count of Nassau (1559-1584), and first stadholder of the United Provinces of the Netherlands, was assassinated by Burgundian Balthasar Gerard (25) with a handgun. Philip II of Spain had called for a volunteer assassin due to William's reluctance take a public stand on religious issues. William was succeeded by his 17-year-old son, Maurice of Nassau.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.
Britain waged wars against the Dutch.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Hurst was born before 13 March 1568, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, her father, William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment, was 38 and her mother, Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer, was 31. She married Thomas Rogers on 18 June 1593, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died from January 1621 to March 1621, in Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Hurst est née avant 13 mars 1568, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Rogers le 18 juin 1593, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée du janvier 1621 au mars 1621, à Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, et a été enterrée à Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Hurst vor 13. März 1568 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment 38 und ihre Mutter Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer 31. Sie hat am 18. Juni 1593 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England Thomas Rogers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb von Januar 1621 bis März 1621 in Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Hurst è nata il prima del 13 marzo 1568 nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, sua padre, William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Rogers il 18 giugno 1593, nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì da gennaio 1621 a marzo 1621, nel Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, e fu sepolta a Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Hurstが1568年3月13日以前にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで生まれた時，父William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parlimentは38歳，母Rose Marshe Agnes Fischerは31歳であった。1593年6月18日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウでThomas Rogersと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1621年1月まで1621年3月にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニープリモウスで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニープリモウスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Hurst(이)가 1568년 3월 13일 이전 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1593년 6월 18일 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 Thomas Rogers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1621년 1월 부터 1621년 3월 까지 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 플림우스에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 플림우스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Hurst nasceu antes 13 de março de 1568, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Rogers em 18 de junho de 1593, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu de janeiro de 1621 a março de 1621, em Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, e foi sepultada em Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Hurst родилась перед 13 марта 1568 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Rogers 18 июня 1593 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла с январь 1621 по марта 1621 в местности плимоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика и была похоронена в местности плимоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Hurst nació antes de 13 de marzo de 1568, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Thomas Rogers el 18 de junio de 1593, en Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió desde enero de 1621 hasta marzo de 1621, en Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, y fue sepultada en Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.
